Program_Description: Healthy Families Del Norte County is an evidence-based home visiting program, accredited by Healthy Families America (HFA). It provides voluntary, free services starting during pregnancy or the first few months of a baby's life.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals or families with infants under 3 months.
Eligibility_Requirements: Be Del Norte County residents; be pregnant or have an infant under 3 months; willing to participate.
Application_Process: Self-referrals are welcome; call or complete the referral form.

Program_Description: The WIC program provides nutritional support and education for women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Healthy Families Del Norte provides support for families with newborns, focusing on stress and risk-factor screening.
Target_Population: Families with newborns up to 3 months

Program_Description: Resources for pregnant and postpartum individuals experiencing mental health challenges.

Maternal Child Adolescent Health (MCAH) Program is made up of a variety of programs designed to improve and promote the health and wellness of women of reproductive age, infants, children, adolescents, and their families.
Target_Population: Women of reproductive age, infants, children, adolescents, and their families

Program_Description: WIC provides nutritional support and education for low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, as well as infants and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Healthy Families Del Norte provides support and resources for families with newborns up to 3 months, focusing on stress and risk-factor screening at birth.
Target_Population: Families with newborns up to 3 months

Program_Description: Healthy Families Del Norte County is an evidence-based home visiting program, accredited by Healthy Families America (HFA). It provides voluntary, free services starting during pregnancy or the first few months of a baby's life.
Target_Population: Families with newborns up to 3 months; pregnant individuals.
Eligibility_Requirements: Be Del Norte County residents; Be pregnant or have an infant under 3 months; Willing to participate.
Application_Process: Self-referrals are welcome; call or complete the referral form.

Program_Description: WIC is a special supplemental nutrition program that provides no-cost services to women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low to medium income families living in California, including families who are pregnant, breastfeeding, who just had a baby (or pregnancy loss), or have children under 5 years old.
Eligibility_Requirements: Newly pregnant individuals, working, military and migrant families may also qualify for the WIC program.

Program_Description: A program that provides parent educator home visits.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases).
